summary: Closed, unmerged automated overnight decomp PR with no changed files or diff. The PR body only reported progress status: 0 matched, 0 failed, and 1 in-progress function, `grPura_80212FC0` in `grpura.c`, marked pending. There is no evidence of code changes, review feedback, or matching tactics in this slice.

key_files.role: Mentioned in the PR progress table as an in-progress pending decomp target; no file diff was present.

what_changed: No files were changed according to the PR context: changed_files count is 0, added_lines is 0, deleted_lines is 0, and diff_bytes is 0.
The PR body recorded automated progress for one pending function: `grPura_80212FC0` in `grpura.c`.
The PR was closed shortly after creation and was not merged.
smart_moves: The automated report separated matched, failed, and in-progress counts, making the run status easy to triage.
The pending target was identified by both source file and function name, which is useful for follow-up decomp work even without a diff.
decomp_lessons: A PR with zero diff can still be useful as a searchable status checkpoint, but it should not be treated as evidence of a successful match.
For future automated decomp runs, include either the generated candidate diff or a clear reason why the pending function produced no committed changes.
When a function remains pending, preserve the file/function pairing so later agents can resume from the same target.
naming_conventions: `grPura_80212FC0` follows the common decomp placeholder convention of subsystem/stage prefix plus address suffix.
`grpura.c` appears to use a lowercase stage/source-file naming style for the Pura stage.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching `grpura.c` or `grPura_80212FC0` to see whether the pending target was eventually matched.
Search for other overnight decomp runs around 2026-03-16 that may contain the actual generated candidate or continuation.
Check whether automated no-diff decomp status PRs are expected behavior or indicate a failed generation step.
